source_snippet:
FAaaS Project Plan Template (AI-Ready)
======================================

How to use this template1. Copy this page for each new project.
2. Fill Section 1 first, especially the FAAASINGES and FAAASPMO parent Jira keys.
3. Update Section 6 Jira queries with the correct parent keys for the project.
4. Keep section names and table column names unchanged so AI agents can parse and update safely.
5. Use plain text status values only from the status enum in Section 2.
flat

1) Project Metadata
-------------------



| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Project Name | FA DB Upgrade from 19c to 26ai |
| Project Type |  Migration |
| TPM Owner | Thomas Jose |
| Dev Lead |  |
| Product Owner |  |
| Primary FAAASPMO Jira | [FAAASPMO-1190](https://jira.oci.oraclecorp.com/browse/FAAASPMO-1190) |
| Primary FAAASINGES Jira | [FAAASINGES-2526](https://jira.oci.oraclecorp.com/browse/FAAASINGES-2526) |
| Jira/Bug Report | [FAAASPMO-1190 - FA DB Upgrade from 19c to 26ai - Bug List](https://confluence.oraclecorp.com/confluence/display/OCIFACP/FAAASPMO-1190+-+FA+DB+Upgrade+from+19c+to+26ai+-+Bug+List) |
| Target GA Date | TBD |
| Current Phase | Design and POC |
| Overall Status | PLANNED |
| Last AI Sync (PT) |  |

| Slack Channel | * FAaaS Internal Development slack channel (overall) - <https://oracle.enterprise.slack.com/archives/C0APKBM1EC9>

* Other Temporary channels:
	+ <https://oracle.enterprise.slack.com/archives/C0AFKNG8FRS>
 |

2) AI Execution Contract
------------------------

This block is machine-readable. Keep keys and section names stable.

yamlai\_execution\_contract.yamltrue3) Objectives and Scope
-----------------------



| Topic | Details |
| --- | --- |
| Executive Summary | Provision new pods running 26ai DB and upgrade the existing ExaCS/ADB fleet to 26ai​ |
| Business Outcome | Provision new pods with 26ai and Upgrade the fleet to 26ai |
| In Scope | Upgrade ExaCS+IDCS and ADB pods |
| Out of Scope | No upgrade planned for ExaCS+IDM pods |
| Dependencies | FRE (Repo, NGFABS etc) |
| Assumptions | Each environment family maintains a single, consistent database version (either 19c or 26ai); both versions are supported during the migration window; 26ai-specific features are deferred until migration completion; and Exa+IDM is excluded from the 26ai requirement.​ |

5) Work Breakdown Structure
---------------------------

Repeatable TPM task baseline. Delete/add/expand rows as needed. **Parent ID is intentionally removed**; hierarchy is represented by Task ID numbering and Dependency.

Dev Jira Tracking RuleDo not copy every Dev Jira story into this WBS. Track execution in Jira and keep WBS at rollout-level:

* Use one WBS row per workstream/phase gate.
* Use **Source Jira** to point to epic/filter/parent ticket.
* Add individual Dev Jira tickets in WBS only when they are cross-team blockers, external dependencies, or leadership-visible risks.

expected_extraction.program_title: FA Database Upgrade from Oracle Database 19c to Oracle Database 26ai
expected_extraction.rag_summary: AMBER — overall status is PLANNED and key external dependencies (FRE repo/template deliverables) have slipped with ETA “to be finalized,” creating schedule risk despite design work being in progress.
expected_extraction.schedule_health: At risk — multiple critical FRE dependency deliverables have no committed dates (ETA moved / to be finalized), which can gate build and rollout sequencing.
expected_extraction.exec_summary: Provision new pods running Oracle Database 26ai and upgrade the existing Exadata Cloud Service (ExaCS) / Autonomous Database (ADB) fleet to 26ai. Scope includes ExaCS+IDCS and ADB pods; ExaCS+IDM is explicitly out of scope. Design documentation and design review/approval are in progress, but schedule is at risk due to external FRE dependencies (starter repos/templates and related Jira items) needing additional time with ETA not yet finalized.
key_milestones: Requirements Locked — target date not specified (Milestone M1, PLANNED).
Design Approved — target date not specified (Milestone M2, PLANNED); design review must include CSSAP LOB, SaaS Security, SUT/Pillar, SaaS Tools, SaaS Ops, Capacity, and external dev teams as needed.
Build Complete — target date not specified (Milestone M3, PLANNED).
Validation Complete — target date not specified (Milestone M4, PLANNED).
GA — target date TBD / not specified (Milestone M5, PLANNED).
External dependency: FRE repo/template deliverables for Starter ADB (26ai & 19c) and related support items (e.g., FRE-18931) — ETA moved / to be finalized (not dated).
External dependency: FRE FA Template Orchestration for STARTER_ADBS (19c + 26ai), 19c-only, and 26ai-only artifacts — ETA moved / not specified.
External dependency: GI Gold Image — Complete (received).
blocking_issues: Program delivery is at risk because critical FRE deliverables (starter ADB/EXA repos, CDRM images, template orchestration, and related Jira items such as FRE-18931) need additional time and the ETA is “to be finalized”; mitigation is Mitigation not stated in source.
next_steps: Complete Dev Design Documentation (Task 2.2) — date not specified in source.
Hold Dev Design Review and obtain approval including CSSAP LOB, SaaS Security, SUT/Pillar, SaaS Tools, SaaS Ops, Capacity, and external dev teams as needed (Task 2.3) — date not specified in source.
Submit capacity requirements for testing and production via the Capacity Planning Process (Task 3.1) — date not specified in source.
Confirm updated ETA from FRE for starter ADB 26ai/19c repo deliverable (Task 5.0; note dated 4/29) — 4/29.
Confirm updated ETA from FRE for FRE-18931 support item (Task 5.01; note dated 4/29) — 4/29.
exec_asks: Align with FRE leadership on a committed delivery date for the starter repo/template deliverables (starter ADB/EXA and template orchestration) because current ETA is “to be finalized” and is gating downstream build/validation; mitigation is Mitigation not stated in source.
expected_extraction.scope_and_impact: Scope is to provision new pods with Oracle Database 26ai and upgrade the existing Exadata Cloud Service (ExaCS)+IDCS and Autonomous Database (ADB) pods to 26ai. Out of scope: no upgrade planned for ExaCS+IDM pods. Assumptions include maintaining a single consistent DB version per environment family (19c or 26ai) while supporting both versions during the migration window, and deferring 26ai-specific features until migration completion.

expected_extraction.program_title: Oracle Database 26ai Upgrade for Exadata Cloud Service (ExaCS) and Autonomous Database Serverless (ADBS) Provisioning/P2T
expected_extraction.rag_summary: AMBER — multiple critical dependencies have slipped (e.g., Storage Factory Change and FA Artifacts need-by moved to 6/10; provisioning ETAs moved from 30-Jun to 10-Aug/15-Aug), and there are stated restrictions (no KMS enablement in prod upgrade cycle) plus significant downtime requirements that increase schedule risk.
expected_extraction.schedule_health: At risk — near-term provisioning and end-to-end P2T depend on shifted need-by items (to 6/10) and unresolved artifact issues, while major non-prod rollout windows (1-Jun to 15-Aug) are constrained by sequencing and readiness requirements.
expected_extraction.exec_summary: Plan focuses on patching Dom0/DomU to 25.2.x (dev 1-May–31-Jul; non-prod rollout 1-Jun–31-Aug) and upgrading Grid Infrastructure (GI) to 26ai with non-prod rollout 1-Jun–15-Aug and prod rollout 1-Sep–31-Dec across ~300 non-prod and ~875 prod clusters. Provisioning-related deliverables have slipped (Storage Factory Change and FA Artifacts need-by moved to 6/10; ExaCS/ADBS provisioning now targets 10-Aug beta and 15-Aug pre-prod), with open issues called out on FA artifact templates and DBMLR/timezone correctness. Upcoming work also includes release management framework updates (starting 19-Jun) and DB upgrade planning that requires 6–8 hours downtime per ExaCS upgrade and excludes KMS enablement during the prod upgrade cycle.
key_milestones: Patch Dom0 to 25.2.x — development 1-May to 31-Jul; non-prod rollout 1-Jun to 31-Aug (Non-Prod: 300 clusters; Prod: 875 clusters).
Patch DomU to 25.2.x — development 1-May to 31-Jul; non-prod rollout 1-Jun to 31-Aug.
Upgrade Grid Infrastructure (GI) to 26ai (23.26) — PoC completed; development 15-May; non-prod rollout 1-Jun to 15-Aug; prod rollout 1-Sep to 31-Dec (Non-Prod: 300 clusters; Prod: 875 clusters).
New ExaCS ingestion with Grid 26ai — development 15-May; non-prod rollout 1-Jun to 15-Aug; from 09/01 onwards all clusters ingested with 26ai GRID.
Release management enablement for 26ai — starts 19-Jun; development 13-Jul to 14-Jul; rollout 20-Jul to 21-Jul (framework + FA template/artifact collection/validations).
PodDB provisioning — dependencies include Storage Factory Change and FA Artifacts (need-by moved to 6/10); targets: ExaCS & ADBS 10-Aug (Starter) beta and 15-Aug pre-prod; ExaCS/ADB CDRM 07-Sep.
P2T end-to-end (ADP & FACP without KMS support) — depends on provisioning and end-to-end provisioning need-by 31-July; targets ExaCS 31-Aug and ADBS 31-Aug.
Upgrade database to 26ai (23.26) including rollback — PoC ETA 31-Jul; requires 6–8 hours downtime for ExaCS; restriction: no KMS enablement as part of prod upgrade cycle for a given pod.
blocking_issues: PodDB provisioning is at risk because key dependencies slipped (Storage Factory Change and FA Artifacts need-by moved from 1-May to 6/10) and there is 'No ETA on FA Artifacts (pdb templates)'; mitigation is Mitigation not stated in source.
Provisioning readiness is at risk because there are issues with 'faimage DBMLR bits (non structured)' and 'timezone files not correct'; mitigation is Mitigation not stated in source.

Production DB upgrade sequencing is constrained because 'For prod, No KMS can be enablement part of Upgrade cycle for a given pod,' which can block pods requiring KMS changes; mitigation is Mitigation not stated in source.

Upgrade rollout success is at risk because GI rollout notes require SUT to start 05/16 to 06/01 and require prioritizing smaller regions plus achieving at least 50% 26ai in bigger regions before supporting provisioning/P2T in prod; mitigation is to prioritize smaller regions first and prioritize clusters with enough free DB units (as stated).

Operational scheduling is at risk because ExaCS upgrade requires 6–8 hours downtime (including rollback support), which can delay rollout if windows are not secured; mitigation is Mitigation not stated in source.
next_steps: Start SUT for GI 26ai rollout between 05/16 and 06/01 (per GI rollout remarks).
Begin non-prod rollout for Dom0/DomU patching on 1-Jun (rollout window 1-Jun to 31-Aug).
Begin non-prod rollout for GI 26ai on 1-Jun (rollout window 1-Jun to 15-Aug).
Close Storage Factory Change and FA Artifacts dependencies by 6/10 (new need-by date) to protect provisioning timelines.
Execute release management work starting 19-Jun (with rollout planned 20-Jul to 21-Jul) to support 26ai in the framework and FA templates/artifact collection.
Target PodDB provisioning beta on 10-Aug (Starter) and pre-prod on 15-Aug for ExaCS and ADBS; prepare for ExaCS/ADB CDRM on 07-Sep.
exec_asks: Approve/secure required downtime windows for ExaCS DB upgrade (6–8 hours per upgrade, including rollback support) to avoid rollout delays.
Prioritize and unblock the slipped dependencies (Storage Factory Change and FA Artifacts) to meet the updated 6/10 need-by date and protect the 10-Aug/15-Aug provisioning targets.
Confirm/endorse the production restriction that KMS enablement cannot be part of the prod upgrade cycle for a pod, and align stakeholders on how KMS will be handled outside the upgrade cycle.
expected_extraction.scope_and_impact: Scope includes patching DBaaS tooling and Dom0/DomU, upgrading Grid Infrastructure (GI) and database to Oracle Database 26ai (23.26), and enabling new ExaCS ingestion with 26ai GRID across ~300 non-prod clusters and ~875 prod clusters. Operational impact includes required upgrade downtime of 6–8 hours for ExaCS (including rollback support) and rollout sequencing constraints (prioritize smaller regions; larger regions need at least 50% at 26ai before provisioning/P2T is supported in prod).
